---
title: "Search Loop"
subtitle: "LLM-guided exploration of the forecasting pipeline"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Try to load search trajectory (may not exist yet)
searchData = {
  try {
    return await FileAttachment("_data/search_trajectory.json").json();
  } catch(e) {
    return [];
  }
}
```

## How the search works

The outer loop uses **Claude** (Sonnet) to propose pipeline configurations. Each iteration:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Claude reads: program.md + history     │
│  → proposes config as JSON              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Quick eval (20 origins, ~30s)          │
│  → if worse: reject immediately         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Full eval (120 origins, ~3min)         │
│  → if better: ACCEPT, update best       │
│  → if worse: REJECT                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Log iteration, save state, continue    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The agent receives domain knowledge (Norway is an oil economy, CPI responds to exchange rates, etc.) and adapts its proposals based on what worked in previous iterations.

## Search trajectory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  if (searchData.length === 0) {
    return html`
      <div class="chart-container" style="text-align:center; padding:3rem;">
        <p style="font-size:1.2rem; color:#666;">No search results yet.</p>
        <p style="color:#999;">Run the search loop to see the trajectory here:</p>
        <code style="background:#f8f9fa; padding:0.5rem 1rem; border-radius:4px;">
          uv run python src/search.py --max-iterations 10
        </code>
      </div>
    `;
  }

  const data = searchData.map(d => ({
    iteration: d.iteration,
    score: d.full_score || d.quick_score,
    status: d.status,
    description: d.description,
    runtime: d.runtime_seconds
  })).filter(d => d.score != null);

  // Running best
  let best = Infinity;
  const bestLine = data.filter(d => d.status === "accepted").map(d => {
    best = Math.min(best, d.score);
    return {iteration: d.iteration, score: best};
  });

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 400,
    marginLeft: 60,
    x: {label: "Iteration"},
    y: {label: "Score (avg MASE)", grid: true},
    marks: [
      // All attempts
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "iteration", y: "score",
        fill: d => d.status === "accepted" ? "#2ecc71" : d.status === "rejected" ? "#e74c3c" : "#f39c12",
        r: 5,
        tip: true,
        title: d => `Iter ${d.iteration}\nScore: ${d.score.toFixed(4)}\nStatus: ${d.status}\n${d.description}`
      }),
      // Best frontier
      Plot.line(bestLine, {
        x: "iteration", y: "score",
        stroke: "#2ecc71", strokeWidth: 2, curve: "step-after"
      }),
    ]
  });
}
```

**Legend:** [Accepted]{style="color:#2ecc71;font-weight:bold"} | [Rejected]{style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:bold"} | [Error]{style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:bold"}. Green line shows the best score achieved so far.


## Iteration log

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  if (searchData.length === 0) {
    return html`<p style="color:#999;">No iterations to display.</p>`;
  }

  return Inputs.table(searchData.map(d => ({
    "#": d.iteration,
    Status: d.status,
    "Quick Score": d.quick_score?.toFixed(4) || "—",
    "Full Score": d.full_score?.toFixed(4) || "—",
    "Time (s)": d.runtime_seconds?.toFixed(1) || "—",
    Description: d.description
  })), {
    rows: 20,
    sort: "#",
    reverse: true
  });
}
```


## What the agent discovers

::: {.callout-note}
This section will be populated after running the search experiment. Key analyses:

- Which covariates appear most frequently in accepted configs?
- What transformations does the agent prefer?
- Does the agent converge or keep exploring?
- Are the discovered configurations economically interpretable?
:::
